text.title: OKR Cascade Multi-Squad Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every multi-squad OKR cascade must enforce. These are HARD rules: founders or squad leads cannot override without explicit retro entry.

rule.statement: Max 3 Objectives at the company level. Max 3 Objectives per squad. Max 5 Key Results per Objective. If you have more, you have not prioritized.
rule.rationale: Three is the upper bound for human working memory across a quarter. Wodtke's case studies show teams with 4+ Os score 0.4 avg vs 0.7 for teams with 1-2 Os. The cascade collapses if company has 7 Os because squads inherit fan-out exceeding their capacity.

rule.statement: Every company-level KR maps to exactly ONE accountable squad. Other squads can be contributors, but only one squad's name appears as Owner. Use RACI Owner column — no shared O.

rationale.source: Doerr, Measure What Matters (2018), ch. 8; Amazon STO ('single-threaded owner') model

rule.rationale: Shared ownership produces the diffusion of responsibility documented by Latane & Darley. If two squads own "lift activation rate to 35%", neither owns it, both blame the other at quarter-end. Single-threaded ownership forces the trade-off conversation up-front.

rule.statement: Every squad OKR document must declare an "Inbound" and "Outbound" dependency list. Inbound = "we need X from squad Y by week N." Outbound = "squad Z needs X from us by week N." Edges that exist in one direction but not the other are bugs — fail the cascade review.

rationale.source: Conway's Law applied to OKRs (Sosa, Eppinger, Rowles, 2004 - design structure matrix)

rule.rationale: Hidden dependencies surface at week 8 as missed quarters. Declaring them in week 1 (a) forces the conversation in cascade week, (b) lets PMs sequence work, (c) makes the dependency graph a living artifact, not a quarter-end excuse.

rule.statement: Every Friday each squad records a confidence-vote per KR on a 0.0-1.0 scale ("how confident are we we'll hit ≥0.7 by quarter-end?"). Drop > 0.2 in a week triggers an escalation conversation Monday — no exception.
rule.rationale: Wodtke's confidence-tracker pattern is the canonical leading indicator. Teams that vote weekly catch 80% of misses by week 6; teams that only check at quarter-end catch zero. The 0.2-drop trigger is calibrated from Wodtke case studies.

rule.statement: Next quarter's company OKR set may not be drafted until previous quarter's per-KR scores (0.0-1.0) are recorded AND a written retro names ≥3 process changes for next cascade. Quarter-end scoring without retro = blocked.

rationale.source: Doerr WMM ch. 11; LinkedIn quarterly OKR retro practice

rule.rationale: Without forced retro, the same drift repeats. The 3-change minimum prevents "looks fine to me" rubber-stamping. Score recording forces the team to confront 0.3 KRs honestly instead of redefining success at quarter-end.

example.title: Company KR → single-squad ownership
example.code:
Company O1: Become the default tool for solo founders post-launch. KR1.1: Activation rate (signup → first published artifact) 18% → 35% Owner: Squad-Onboarding (single owner) Contributors: Squad-Pricing, Squad-Platform KR1.2: Net new MRR from organic +$12k → +$28k Owner: Squad-Growth Contributors: Squad-Content
Squad-Onboarding then cascades KR1.1 into: - "Reduce signup→first-publish median time 14d → 3d" (measurable) - "Cut signup form fields from 9 to 3" (directional, lever)
example.note: Illustrates r2: KR1.1 has one Owner; r3 emerges naturally: Squad-Pricing knows it owes onboarding pricing-page changes by week 4.

example.title: Shared ownership, no dependency declaration
example.code:
KR1.1: Activation rate 18% → 35% Owners: Squad-Onboarding AND Squad-Platform AND Squad-Growth
Problem: violates r2 (three Owners = no Owner) and r3 (no Inbound/Outbound list anywhere). At week 8 Squad-Onboarding blames Squad-Platform for not shipping the new event-tracking pipeline; Platform points at Growth. Quarter-end score: 0.3, all three teams' fault. Repair: assign one Owner. Other two appear in Inbound list with a date.
example.note: Illustrates r2 + r3 violation pattern that recurs every quarter without enforcement.
